ReleaseLabel: Extension 0.34.0 + ai_router 0.27.0 (Set 077 'Lightweight Tier UX and Copilot Hardening')
Notes: Ad-hoc per-set UAT for Set 077 gating BOTH artifacts of the coordinated release: extension 0.34.0 (tier truth chain, three-way Getting Started choice + Python prerequisite, self-completing out-of-band verification prompts, owed-verification banner, Mode-B auto-routing/skew message) and dabbler-ai-router 0.27.0 (resolved-runtime-mode close gate, external-verification round parser incl. WAIVED, Mode-B engine-or-provider gate + start-time same-pair refusal, A12 backfill). REWRITTEN (2026-07-04) to the Set 078 authoring bar after the operator suspended the first walk mid-session over unclear steps ('I couldn't figure out how to run this / I don't see a pending verification banner'): every HumanAction below is now a literal, copy-pasteable command or exact UI element label (never a paraphrase like 'open the form and pick tier choice 1'), every Expectation is a literal string/value to diff against (never 'confirm it looks right'), and every literal string was re-grounded against CURRENT source during this rewrite -- two claims in the original checklist had already drifted from source (Walk 3's button label, and a false claim that the auto-opened doc's callout differs between dedicated-sessions and out-of-band-or-none; it does not -- the callout is tier-only) and are corrected below. UAT runs against a LOCALLY BUILT VSIX ONLY (`npx vsce package`, then Install from VSIX) -- never a published build (the Set 058 lesson). Walks 8, 10, 11, and 12 (the pending-verification banner and the Mode-B start-time gate) are pure Python/CLI features with no webview surface at all; each was re-run live by the orchestrator just now in a disposable scratch fixture (build recipe inlined in the walk) and the Expectation quotes the exact, freshly-captured output byte-for-byte -- this replaces the old checklist's dependence on a fragile multi-walk VS Code scaffold chain for a feature that never needed VS Code to reproduce. Walks 1-7, 9, 13, 14 involve the Getting Started webview and the Session Set Explorer, which cannot be driven headless from this environment; for those, every literal string (button labels, warning copy, marker values, prompt text) was re-verified against current source moments ago, and every Layer 2 (mocha, vscode-stub) test that pins the same behavior was re-run fresh (not cited from memory) -- exact pass counts and output are quoted in each ProgrammaticVerification. What remains genuinely operator-only in those walks is named explicitly per walk (real webview teardown/reload persistence, real right-click menu navigation, real clipboard/status-bar behavior) -- the same honest split as the Set 058 lesson this Notes block already carried, just now enforced walk-by-walk rather than asserted once at the top. Explorer-row items use the disposable fixture workspace from `npm run make-uat-workspace` (the generated copy pins dabblerSessionSets.pythonPath to the repo .venv; mutating actions are safe -- the copy is throwaway; use the view refresh button if the tree does not auto-refresh). Scaffold walks (2-7) each use a FRESH EMPTY FOLDER (one per walk) so the durable .dabbler/ markers and generated docs are written from nothing. Row-state references are the Set 077 fixtures documented in tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/test-fixtures/uat-matrix/README.md.

Review.Subarea: Walk 1 -- make-uat-workspace matrix sweep
Review.ItemLabel: Owed-state rows read 'verification owed' / 'remediation owed' in words; N/M+ fraction and quiet rows behave per the fixture matrix
Review.HumanAction: Run `npm run make-uat-workspace` (from tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration), open the printed uat-matrix.code-workspace, and inspect these rows under hello-world-lightweight: 003-greet-with-note, 004-add-a-farewell, 005-shout-the-greeting, 006-whisper-mode, 007-echo-the-greeting.
Review.Expectation: 005-shout-the-greeting: row description contains the words 'verification owed', fraction reads 2/2+ (the '+' suffix present -- the denominator can still grow). 007-echo-the-greeting: row description contains the words 'remediation owed', fraction reads 3/4 with NO '+' (a typed session already grew the count, so the denominator is fixed). 004-add-a-farewell: fraction reads 1/2+ (still growable), no verification marker. 003-greet-with-note: no verification marker at all and no nag -- an out-of-band record already exists, so the row stays quiet. 006-whisper-mode: fraction reads plain 3/3, no marker -- a completed VERIFIED verification is quiet by design (quiet is success, not absence of coverage).
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: Orchestrator ran `npm run test:unit -- --grep "uat-matrix"` fresh just now: 16/16 passing, including the exact per-row assertions above (uatMatrixFixtures.test.ts, e.g. '005 Mode B work-complete: 2/2 with the + suffix AND v+ (verification owed)', '007 Mode B remediation in flight: remediation owed'). Also ran `npm run test:unit -- --grep "tierLegibility"`-adjacent suites: 26/26 passing, pinning the row-text/marker/fraction rules in tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/utils/tierLegibility.ts and src/providers/SessionSetsModel.ts:195-196 ('verification owed' / 'remediation owed' literal strings). Both suites drive the real readSessionSets scan against the committed fixture tree; the rendered webview text itself (as opposed to the derived data) is the operator portion.

Review.Subarea: Walk 2 -- Full-tier fresh scaffold
Review.ItemLabel: Full choice survives hide/re-expand and window reload; markers, decomposition prompt, and auto-opened doc all say Full
Review.HumanAction: Open a FRESH EMPTY FOLDER. Open the Session Set Explorer's Getting Started form. Click the radio button labeled exactly "Full". Collapse/hide the view and re-expand it; then run 'Developer: Reload Window'. Re-open the form and confirm the "Full" radio is still checked (not reverted, no re-shown warning). Click the button labeled exactly "Build project structure". Then: (a) open `.dabbler/tier` -- it must contain the single word `full`; (b) open `.dabbler/verification-mode` -- it must contain `out-of-band-or-none` (Full always writes this default marker, even though Full has no verification-mode UI of its own); (c) click the button labeled exactly "Copy prompt to build session sets" and paste the clipboard somewhere readable; (d) look at the auto-opened getting-started doc's very first blockquote.
Review.Expectation: `.dabbler/tier` contains exactly `full` (plus trailing newline). `.dabbler/verification-mode` contains exactly `out-of-band-or-none`. The copied prompt contains the literal sentence "The operator selected the **full** tier for this project — author each new set with `tier: full`" and an exemplar spec block containing `tier: full`. The auto-opened doc's first blockquote reads exactly: "> **Your project is set up for the Full tier** (recorded in `.dabbler/tier`). Step 1's tier descriptions below cover both options for reference — the **Lightweight-tier** setup notes do not apply to this project. To change tiers, right-click a session set in the Session Set Explorer and use **Switch Tier…**."
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: Orchestrator ran `npm run test:unit -- --grep "restoreGsState"` fresh just now: 18/18 passing -- covers the exact seed/dirty/reload precedence contract (e.g. 'a DIRTY persisted radio (explicit operator flip) beats the SAME seed', 'Set 076 replay: a Lightweight pick never snaps back to Full') in gettingStartedHtml.js:180-239. Also ran the sessionGenPrompt suite (`npm run test:unit -- --grep "buildSessionGenPrompt"`): 20/20 passing, pinning the exact prompt wording quoted above (src/wizard/sessionGenPrompt.ts:164-207). The doc callout string is a pure function (gettingStartedDoc.ts:71-81, `tierCalloutMarkdown`) quoted verbatim from current source. Marker write-through is unconditional per gitScaffold.ts:114-131. Live reload persistence and real doc rendering in an actual VS Code window are the operator portion -- Layer 2 runs on a vscode-stub with no real webview host.

Review.Subarea: Walk 3 -- Lightweight + dedicated verification fresh scaffold
Review.ItemLabel: Lightweight/dedicated choice is durable; markers and prompt reflect dedicated-sessions mode (doc callout is tier-only, see correction)
Review.HumanAction: Open a SECOND FRESH EMPTY FOLDER. Click the radio labeled "Lightweight". A second radio group appears labeled "Verification (per session set)"; click the option labeled "Dedicated verification sessions" (the option whose description reads: structured verification sessions run on a different AI engine or provider, with a close-out gate). Hide/re-expand the view, then 'Developer: Reload Window', re-open the form, and confirm BOTH the Lightweight radio and the Dedicated-verification radio are still checked. Click "Build project structure". Inspect: (a) `.dabbler/tier` = `lightweight`; (b) `.dabbler/verification-mode` = `dedicated-sessions`; (c) click "Copy prompt to build session sets" and confirm the clipboard contains `tier: lightweight` and the sentence "The operator selected **dedicated verification sessions** for this project — author each Lightweight set with `verificationMode: dedicated-sessions`".
Review.Expectation: Both markers write through as `lightweight` + `dedicated-sessions`, surviving both the re-expand and the reload. The decomposition prompt seeds `verificationMode: dedicated-sessions` in its exemplar plus the guidance sentence quoted above. CORRECTION from the prior version of this checklist: the auto-opened doc's callout is NOT distinct per verification mode -- `tierCalloutMarkdown(tier)` (gettingStartedDoc.ts:71-81) takes only the tier (`"full"` or `"lightweight"`), never the verification mode, so this walk's doc callout is byte-identical to Walk 4's. Do not expect separate 'dedicated' vs 'out-of-band' doc copy anywhere -- there isn't any.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: Orchestrator ran the same `restoreGsState` suite fresh (18/18 passing, includes the 'restoreGsState verification-mode seed (Set 077 S3)' sub-suite covering exactly this seed+reload contract for verificationMode) and re-ran `buildSessionGenPrompt` (20/20 passing, including the dedicated-sessions exemplar + guidance-sentence assertions) moments ago. The tier-only nature of `tierCalloutMarkdown` was confirmed by reading its current signature directly (gettingStartedDoc.ts:71), which takes a single `Tier` parameter -- there is no verificationMode parameter to source a distinct callout from.

Review.Subarea: Walk 4 -- Lightweight + out-of-band/none fresh scaffold
Review.ItemLabel: Lightweight/out-of-band choice is durable; markers reflect out-of-band-or-none mode; the canonical verification doc is present (but unconditionally, not specific to this walk -- see correction)
Review.HumanAction: Open a THIRD FRESH EMPTY FOLDER. Click "Lightweight", then confirm the radio labeled "Out-of-band or none" (description: copy a review prompt into a second AI assistant and record its verdict by hand) is already checked by default -- this is the pre-selected option, you should not need to click it. Hide/re-expand, 'Developer: Reload Window', re-open the form, confirm the picks held. Click "Build project structure". Inspect: (a) `.dabbler/tier` = `lightweight`; (b) `.dabbler/verification-mode` = `out-of-band-or-none`; (c) confirm `docs/dabbler/cross-provider-verification.md` exists in the generated repo.
Review.Expectation: Markers write through as `lightweight` + `out-of-band-or-none`. CORRECTION from the prior version of this checklist: `docs/dabbler/cross-provider-verification.md` is NOT specific to the out-of-band choice -- `CROSS_PROVIDER_VERIFICATION_REL_PATH` (consumerBootstrap.ts:382-386) is written unconditionally by the scaffold's file map on every tier and every verification-mode combination (Full included, dedicated-sessions included), so its presence here confirms the scaffold ran, not that this specific mode was chosen. As in Walk 3, the auto-opened doc's callout is identical to Walk 3's -- tier-only, not mode-specific.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: Orchestrator re-ran the `restoreGsState` suite fresh (18/18 passing) for the seed/dirty/reload contract shared by all three tier-choice walks. The unconditional nature of the cross-provider-verification doc write was confirmed by reading consumerBootstrap.ts's file-map object literals directly (both `renderConsumerBootstrap` at :432-441 and `renderStructureBootstrap` at :484-493 include the same key with no tier/mode branch) -- no dedicated Layer 2 test isolates this specific question, so this is a source-grounded claim, not a freshly-run assertion; flagging that distinction explicitly per this rewrite's own bar.

Review.Subarea: Walk 5 -- tier-leak stress on a live pick
Review.ItemLabel: An explicitly flipped radio (tierDirty) is never silently reverted by a later seed
Review.HumanAction: In a fresh folder (no `.dabbler/tier` written yet) open the Getting Started form -- "Full" is checked by default since no durable seed exists. Click the radio labeled "Lightweight" (this sets the in-memory `tierDirty` flag and persists it via the webview's own state, without contacting the extension host). Hide/re-expand the view WITHOUT reloading. Then run 'Developer: Reload Window' (a full extension-host + webview restart). Re-open the form.
Review.Expectation: The Lightweight pick persists across BOTH the re-expand and the reload -- it does not snap back to Full. Because no scaffold has run yet in this fresh folder, there is no durable `.dabbler/tier` seed to contend with, so the flipped choice simply survives untouched. No warning about the Full tier re-appears over the Lightweight pick.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: CORRECTION from the prior version of this checklist: its citation ('tierMarkerStore.test.ts + gettingStartedActions.test.ts cover... the tierDirty record-over-seed rule') is factually wrong -- grepping both files for the literal string `tierDirty` returns zero matches, confirmed just now. The real coverage is gettingStartedHtml.test.ts's 'restoreGsState (Set 077 S2)' suite, re-run fresh: 18/18 passing, including 'a DIRTY persisted radio (explicit operator flip) beats the SAME seed', 'a CHANGED seed... overrides a dirty flip and clears it', and 'Set 076 replay: a Lightweight pick never snaps back to Full' -- the exact scenario this walk exercises. The real `vscode.setState`/`getState` round-trip across an actual window reload is inherently untestable on the vscode-stub and remains the operator portion.

Review.Subarea: Walk 6 -- Python prerequisite warning on the form
Review.ItemLabel: Step-1 Python warning appears when no interpreter resolves, on both tiers
Review.HumanAction: In a fresh folder, set the `dabblerSessionSets.pythonPath` setting to an empty or invalid value, and ensure no `python`/`python3` resolves on PATH for this VS Code window. Open the Getting Started form and look at the very top of step 1 -- above the tier radio buttons.
Review.Expectation: A warning banner (role="alert") renders this exact text: "Python was not found on this machine. Build project structure creates a virtual environment, which still needs a base Python install. Install Python from python.org (tick \"Add python.exe to PATH\"; avoid the Microsoft Store build), or set the dabblerSessionSets.pythonPath setting to an installed interpreter. Then reload the VS Code window (changes made after launch are not visible until you reload)." This warning renders identically regardless of which tier radio is selected (Lightweight is router-off, not Python-off) and appears BEFORE the tier radio group, not after. It clears once a valid interpreter resolves (checked in order: the explicit setting, then a workspace `.venv`, then bare `python`/`python3` on PATH).
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: Orchestrator ran `npm run test:unit -- --grep "A10 missing-Python warning|pythonPresent"` fresh just now: 9/9 passing, including 'pythonWarningHtml renders role=alert and flips hidden on visible', 'warning VISIBLE when pythonPresent is false — on BOTH tiers', and 'warning leads step 1 (renders before the tier radios)' (gettingStartedHtml.js:96-103 for the exact string, :415/:420 for the visibility/ordering logic). The real host-side probe against a genuinely broken PATH (as opposed to the fake-fs-driven unit test) is the operator portion.

Review.Subarea: Walk 7 -- Python pre-flight fails friendly, leaves nothing behind
Review.ItemLabel: Build with no interpreter fails BEFORE any durable write with a friendly explainer and no setup artifacts
Review.HumanAction: With the interpreter still unresolved (from Walk 6), in a fresh empty folder click the button labeled "Build project structure". Read the error notification, then inspect the folder's contents.
Review.Expectation: An error notification appears with exactly this text: "Build project structure needs a Python interpreter, but none was found — no Python is installed, or it is not on PATH. This is a missing Python installation, NOT an extension or API-key problem. Install Python from https://www.python.org/downloads/ (tick \"Add python.exe to PATH\"; avoid the Microsoft Store build), or point the 'dabblerSessionSets.pythonPath' setting at an installed interpreter, then reload the VS Code window and try again." The folder is left COMPLETELY EMPTY -- no `.dabbler/`, no generated docs, no venv, nothing at all (the pre-flight check runs before git init, before any marker write, before any template rendering).
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: Orchestrator ran `npm run test:unit -- --grep "Python pre-flight"` fresh just now: 1/1 passing -- gitScaffoldCore.test.ts's 'gitScaffold — Python pre-flight leaves no artifacts (Set 077 S3, M7)' asserts `fs.readdirSync(tmpDir)` deep-equals `[]` after a simulated missing-interpreter build attempt, with the message 'pre-flight failure must leave no setup artifacts'. The pre-flight-runs-first ordering was confirmed by reading gitScaffold.ts directly: the interpreter probe is the first statement of `buildProjectStructureNoPrompt`, before git init and before any marker write. NOTE: a separate operator question about `Dabbler: install ai-router` leaving only `budget.yaml` in a fresh venv is a DIFFERENT command from this walk's pre-flight check and was not investigated as part of this rewrite -- flagging it as still open, not resolved by this pass.

Review.Subarea: Walk 8 -- banner fires BEFORE the artifact (self-contained CLI reproduction)
Review.ItemLabel: A completed Mode-A (out-of-band) set with no external-verification.md makes the NEXT session's start_session print a pending-verification banner naming it
Review.HumanAction:
In a terminal at the repo root with the project's `.venv` active, pick any empty throwaway folder as PARENT (e.g. `C:\temp\uat8\session-sets`) and run all of the following:
1. `mkdir "PARENT/001-first-set"`
2. Write `PARENT/001-first-set/spec.md` containing exactly these 9 lines (the ```yaml fence below is part of the real file content, not a formatting wrapper -- copy it as shown):
# spec
[blank line]
## Session Set Configuration
[blank line]
```yaml
tier: lightweight
verificationMode: out-of-band-or-none
```
[blank line]
### Session 1 of 1: Do the work
3. Write `PARENT/001-first-set/session-state.json` containing exactly:
`{"schemaVersion": 4, "sessionSetName": "001-first-set", "status": "complete", "sessions": [{"number": 1, "title": "Do the work", "status": "complete", "startedAt": "2026-07-04T09:00:00-04:00", "completedAt": "2026-07-04T09:30:00-04:00", "orchestrator": {"engine": "claude-code", "provider": "anthropic"}, "verificationVerdict": null}]}`
4. Write `PARENT/001-first-set/activity-log.json` containing exactly: `{"entries": []}`
5. Run: `.venv/Scripts/python.exe -c "import dedicated_verification as dv; dv.resolve_and_record_verification_mode(r'PARENT/001-first-set', cli_choice=dv.VERIFICATION_MODE_OUT_OF_BAND)"`
6. `mkdir "PARENT/002-second-set"` and write a spec.md there identical in shape to step 2's (any session title).
7. Run: `.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m ai_router.start_session --session-set-dir "PARENT/002-second-set" --engine claude-code --provider anthropic --no-router`
Review.Expectation:
Step 7 prints exactly this banner to the terminal, naming `001-first-set` by name and pointing at its exact `external-verification.md` path:
```
[dabbler] ====================================================================
[dabbler] PENDING VERIFICATION - action needed:
[dabbler]   001-first-set: external verification owed - paste an 'Evaluate ...' prompt (Session Sets view -> Copy Prompt) into a DIFFERENT assistant and have it write its verdict to <PARENT-absolute-path>/001-first-set/external-verification.md
[dabbler] ====================================================================
```
Only the absolute path varies (wherever you created PARENT); every other word must match verbatim. This is a from-scratch, no-VS-Code-needed reproduction of the exact scenario the operator got stuck on last time ('I couldn't figure out how to run this / I don't see a pending verification banner').
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: Orchestrator ran this exact 7-step recipe live moments ago and captured this exact banner text verbatim (ai_router/pending_verification.py:290-305 `format_banner` + :164-195 `_mode_a_owed_notice` generate this precise wording). test_pending_verification.py's Mode-A owed-notice tests cover the derivation mechanically against fixtures; this walk additionally confirms the real `start_session` CLI entry point prints it to a live process, not just that the pure function returns the right string.

Review.Subarea: Walk 9 -- Copilot persona writes the artifact itself
Review.ItemLabel: Pasting the Evaluate prompt in a second chat under a DIFFERENT provider makes the engine write a templated, dated, verdict-bearing external-verification.md
Review.HumanAction: In a real (non-scratch) Lightweight + out-of-band repo with a pending-verification notice (e.g. from Walk 8's real recipe applied to an actual project, or any project with an owed set), in the Dabbler Session Sets view right-click the owed set's row -> Copy Prompt -> Evaluate Session Set. Open a SECOND chat under a DIFFERENT provider than the one that did the work (e.g. if the work was done under Claude/anthropic, switch the second chat's model picker to an openai or google model). Paste the copied prompt and let the engine act. Then open `docs/session-sets/<slug>/external-verification.md`.
Review.Expectation:
The engine ITSELF writes the file (the extension never parses verdicts). The prompt you pasted opens with the literal line "Cross-provider review request (out-of-band verification)." followed by "First read `docs/dabbler/cross-provider-verification.md` (repo root)" and closes with the literal sentence "Non-negotiable final step: YOU (the reviewing engine) must write your verdict as a new dated round section appended to `docs/session-sets/<slug>/external-verification.md` (UTF-8, append-only — never rewrite earlier rounds)." If the extension had to seed the file first, its template is exactly: "# External Verification — <setName>

> Out-of-band verification record for this session set. Reviewing
> engines append one dated round section each; the latest round wins.
> Instructions + verdict grammar: docs/dabbler/cross-provider-verification.md

## Round 1 — <YYYY-MM-DD>

Verdict: PENDING
". The reviewing engine's job is to replace `Verdict: PENDING` with a real verdict (VERIFIED, or ISSUES_FOUND with [Critical]/[Major]/[Minor]-tagged findings, or WAIVED with a one-line reason) in that Round N section, or append a new Round N section for a later round.
Review.NoProgrammaticPathReason: Judgment item: whether a live chat under a different provider actually reads the doc and produces a well-formed, on-template artifact is a qualitative round-trip no automated suite can stand in for (the parser tests exercise fixture files, not a live model). The quoted opener/closer/template strings were re-grounded against current source during this rewrite (tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/commands/copyPromptCommands.ts:113-159, src/commands/externalVerification.ts:23-38, confirmed by re-running `npm run test:unit -- --grep "carries set name, dated Round"` -- 1/1 passing).

Review.Subarea: Walk 10 -- soft gate recognizes the verdict; banner clears
Review.ItemLabel: Once external-verification.md carries a recognizable verdict, the pending-verification banner stops firing for that set
Review.HumanAction:
Continuing the Walk 8 scratch fixture:
1. Write `PARENT/001-first-set/external-verification.md` containing exactly these 7 lines:
# External verification
[blank line]
## Round 1 -- 2026-07-04
[blank line]
Verdict: VERIFIED
[blank line]
Reviewed by a different provider; no issues found.
2. `mkdir "PARENT/003-third-set"` with its own spec.md (same shape as before).
3. Run: `.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m ai_router.start_session --session-set-dir "PARENT/003-third-set" --engine claude-code --provider anthropic --no-router`
Review.Expectation: Step 3 prints NOTHING about `001-first-set` -- no `[dabbler] PENDING VERIFICATION` block at all -- contrast with Walk 8, where the identical command against a fresh sibling printed the banner. This confirms the content-aware soft gate: once a recognizable verdict (VERIFIED here) exists, the set stops being nagged. Separately, at real `close_session` time (not reproduced in this scratch fixture -- that requires a real git-committed session set with a `disposition.json`, outside a minimal scratch fixture's reach), the gate prints the literal line "external-verification.md verdict: VERIFIED (round 1)" when a VERIFIED verdict is present, or a WARNING naming the soft-gate guidance when none is found.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: Orchestrator ran steps 1-3 live moments ago: with `001-first-set/external-verification.md` absent (Walk 8), the identical `start_session` command printed the banner; after adding the VERIFIED verdict file and re-running for a new sibling, the banner did not print -- a real, freshly-captured before/after comparison, not cited from a prior run. The close_session soft-gate wording is grounded directly in close_session.py:1883-1908 and covered by test_no_router_close_session.py's content-aware soft-gate assertions (not independently re-run this pass, since reaching a real close_session invocation requires a git-committed set with disposition.json -- out of scope for this scratch fixture).

Review.Subarea: Walk 11 -- single-engine Copilot gate PASSES on provider difference
Review.ItemLabel: Work under engine=copilot/provider=anthropic + verification under engine=copilot/provider=openai: no start-time refusal, close gate passes
Review.HumanAction:
In the same repo as Walk 12, right after its refusal (which made no write), run:
`.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m ai_router.start_session --session-set-dir "<set-dir>" --type verification --engine copilot --provider openai --no-router`
Review.Expectation:
The command SUCCEEDS (exit code 0) and prints exactly:
```
[dabbler] Session 2 is a VERIFICATION session (type=verification).
[dabbler] Typed sessions take their step list from docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md -> Lightweight dedicated verification (NOT a spec.md heading).
```
`session-state.json` gains a new session 2 entry with `type: verification` and `orchestrator {engine: copilot, provider: openai}` -- the start-time refusal does NOT fire because the provider differs even though the engine matches (the sanctioned single-engine Copilot pattern). Calling `ai_router.dedicated_verification.validate_dedicated_verification(set_dir, closing_session_number=2)` at this point returns `applicable=True, ok=True` -- the close-time gate would pass.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: Orchestrator built this exact fixture and ran this exact `start_session` command live moments ago: it succeeded, printed exactly the two lines above, and appended session 2 with the copilot/openai identity to `session-state.json`. Then called `validate_dedicated_verification` directly against the resulting real state file (not a mock) and confirmed `applicable=True, ok=True`. test_cross_provider_gate_extension.py + test_dedicated_verification_close_gate.py cover the same predicate on fixtures.

Review.Subarea: Walk 12 -- same engine + same provider is REFUSED at start
Review.ItemLabel: Verification start with a pair matching every work session is refused before any write
Review.HumanAction:
In a Lightweight + dedicated-sessions repo with one completed work session recorded as `--engine copilot --provider anthropic` (do this BEFORE Walk 11's step, on the same fixture -- Walk 11 adds a passing verification session afterward), attempt:
`.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m ai_router.start_session --session-set-dir "<set-dir>" --type verification --engine copilot --provider anthropic --no-router`
Review.Expectation:
The command FAILS with exit code 3 and prints exactly this message (no `session-state.json` write occurs -- re-reading the file afterward shows only the original single work session, nothing appended):
"start_session: refused -- the declared verification identity (engine='copilot', provider='anthropic') does not differ from the work sessions by engine or by provider, so the Mode-B close-out gate could never pass it. Cross-provider review is the point of a dedicated verification session. Fix: run it on a different engine, or keep the engine and switch the model provider - the sanctioned single-engine pattern for a Copilot-locked shop is a second Copilot chat with the model picker on another provider, declared as: --engine copilot --provider openai (verifying work done under --engine copilot --provider anthropic). Declare the true provider with --provider; if the work sessions' providers were never recorded, record them on the per-session orchestrator blocks first."
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: Orchestrator ran this exact command live moments ago against the exact fixture and confirmed this exact refusal message and exit code 3, then re-read `session-state.json` and confirmed it was unchanged (no session 2 written). test_cross_provider_gate_extension.py's start-time same-pair refusal test covers this mechanically on a fixture.

Review.Subarea: Walk 13 -- new extension + pre-0.27.0 router fails LOUD
Review.HumanAction: With the locally built extension 0.34.0 installed, point the workspace's Python environment at a PRE-0.27.0 `dabbler-ai-router` (e.g. `pip install dabbler-ai-router==0.26.2` in the workspace venv). Copy a verification-kickoff or remediation-handoff prompt (row action "Start Next Session" on a row that derives to awaiting-verification or awaiting-remediation) and read it.
Review.Expectation: The copied prompt contains this exact line: "This flow expects dabbler-ai-router >= 0.27.0 (the start-time cross-provider guardrail and the owed-verification banner ship there). (`MODE_B_MIN_ROUTER_VERSION = "0.27.0"` has not changed since Set 077 introduced it, confirmed via `git log -S` on the constant -- unaffected by Set 078 separately bumping `pyproject.toml` to 0.28.0, since 078 made no extension changes.)
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: Orchestrator re-ran `npm run test:unit -- --grep "0.27.0"` fresh just now: passing -- copyPromptCommands.test.ts's 'surfaces the minimum router version (critique M6 version-skew)' asserts the live `buildVerificationKickoffPrompt` output includes exactly this string (copyPromptCommands.ts:333-343). The actual cross-version failure against a genuinely pre-0.27.0 installed router package is the operator portion -- reproducing it would require pip-installing an older `dabbler-ai-router` into the working venv, which this pass intentionally did not do to avoid mutating the repo's own environment.
